items.question: The maximum likelihood estimator θ̂ satisfies:
items.explanation: MLE maximizes the likelihood L(θ) = P(data | θ), not the posterior P(θ | data).

items.question: For i.i.d. observations, the log-likelihood is:
items.explanation: log of a product equals sum of logs: log ∏ᵢ P(xᵢ|θ) = Σᵢ log P(xᵢ|θ).

items.question: Bernoulli MLE for p after observing 7 heads in 10 flips is:
Minimizing negative log-likelihood is equivalent to:
items.explanation: −ℓ is minimized ⟺ ℓ is maximized ⟺ L is maximized (log is increasing).

items.question: Gaussian MLE for μ with known σ and n observations is:
items.explanation: ℓ(μ) ∝ −Σ(xᵢ−μ)² is maximized at μ̂ = x̄.
